title: E10.W5 Run large synthetic project dogfood

AC1_50plus_epics_500plus_tasks.details: Saga T001 created with 55 member epics (T002-T056) and 550 tasks (T0200-T0749). Exceeds minimums of 50+ epics and 500+ tasks.

AC2_pagination_budget_rollup_performance.details: All operations perform acceptably at scale: saga rollup ~2.7s, saga members ~2.1s, list --parent ~2.2s, focus ~2.7s, show ~2.7s, briefing ~2.7s, orchestrate ready ~2.9s, orchestrate waves ~2.8s. 550 ready tasks reported correctly. 1 wave with all tasks.

AC3_graph_validate_usable.details: cleo graph status works (index freshness). cleo graph shape-check works for route validation. Graph subsystem responds correctly at 606-node scale. Full WorkGraph validate (T10632 feature) is cleocode-dev only, not yet in global CLEO v2026.5.122. Graph status/discovery/search all functional.

warnings: cleo sql command not available in global CLEO v2026.5.122
cleo graph validate not available in global CLEO (cleocode-dev feature T10632)
add-batch requires acceptance as JSON array, not pipe-separated string
Saga type='saga' from API but DB stores type='epic' with labels_json=['saga']
Strict lifecycle mode blocks saga creation; advisory mode workaround used
BRAIN duplicate-check on add-batch adds ~60s per batch at 550-task scale
cleo dist nuke race: local cleo.js build can lose dist between calls; global cleo stable
validation.recommendation: PM-Core V2 handles 55-epic/550-task projects correctly. Performance is acceptable (~2.7s per operation). Key findings: add-batch needs array acceptance format, strict mode blocks sagas, and BRAIN duplicate checks are costly at scale.
